title: Severe Heatwave in Bihar: 11 Districts Above 40°C, Patna Likely to Touch 41°C Today - Patna Press
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Bihar
full_text:
Patna: Heatwave conditions tightened their grip across Bihar on Monday, with temperatures crossing 40°C in at least eight districts. Dehri in Rohtas district recorded the highest temperature in the state at 43.2°C, followed closely by Kaimur at 43.0°C, according to official data.
In the state capital Patna, the maximum temperature was recorded at 40°C, which is 2.2 degrees above normal. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that temperatures may rise further, with Patna likely to touch 41°C on Tuesday amid strong sunlight and hot winds.
School Timings Revised Amid Rising Heat
In view of the extreme weather conditions, Patna District Magistrate Dr. Chandrashekhar Singh has ordered a change in school timings for younger students. From April 21 to April 25, all pre-schools, anganwadi centres, and classes up to Grade 5 — in both government and private institutions — will function only till 12:30 pm.
Storm and Rain Alerts Issued
The IMD has forecast gusty winds with speeds of 50–60 kmph in districts including Araria, Kishanganj, Purnia, and Katihar on April 21. Additionally, rainfall has been predicted in several parts of north and south-eastern Bihar on April 24 and 25, offering possible temporary relief from the heat.
Heatwave Impacts: Students Fall Ill
The soaring temperatures have already begun to impact daily life. In Buxar, at least 10 students reportedly fainted due to extreme heat and were rushed to a hospital for treatment. The district recorded temperatures close to 42°C on Monday. Heatwave-like conditions were reported in at least five districts across the state.
In Jamui, a student’s health deteriorated due to the heat, forcing school authorities to make the child lie down on a bench. Similar scenes were witnessed in other districts, highlighting the severity of the situation.
Widespread Heat Across State
Over the past 24 hours, 11 districts in Bihar recorded maximum temperatures of 40°C or above. Apart from Rohtas and Kaimur, several regions continue to experience intense heat, with little relief from winds.
In Nalanda, the temperature is expected to reach 41°C, with a minimum of around 26°C. Wind speeds remain low at approximately 5 kmph, contributing to uncomfortable conditions.
Visuals Reflect Harsh Conditions
Images from across the state show deserted roads during peak afternoon hours in Madhubani, while residents in Rohtas were seen covering their heads with cloth to shield themselves from the scorching sun.
Advisory Issued
The weather department has advised children, elderly citizens, and those with health conditions to take precautions and avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours unless necessary, as the heatwave conditions are expected to persist in the coming days.

title: School timings revised up to Class 5 in Patna: Mercury crosses 40°C in several districts; temperatures may ... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
School timings revised up to Class 5 in Patna:Mercury crosses 40°C in several districts; temperatures may touch 45°C in the next 2 days
- Copy link
A sharp rise in temperatures across Bihar has prompted authorities in Patna to revise school timings for younger students, even as several districts reported maximum temperatures above 40°C on Monday.
According to official data, at least eight districts recorded temperatures beyond the 40°C mark. Dehri in Rohtas district emerged as the hottest location at 43.2°C, while the capital Patna registered a maximum of 40°C, 2.2 degrees above normal.
Timings changed for primary classes
In view of the prevailing heatwave conditions, Patna District Magistrate Dr Tyagarajan has ordered a change in school hours for children up to Class 5. Between April 21 and April 25, pre-schools, Anganwadi centres, and schools up to Class 5 will function only till 12:30 p.m.
The directive applies to both government and private institutions and is aimed at minimising children’s exposure to extreme afternoon heat.
Some pictures...
Storm warning and rain forecast
The India Meteorological Department has forecast strong winds with speeds of 50–60 kmph in districts such as Araria, Kishanganj, Purnia, and Katihar on April 21.
Additionally, rainfall has been predicted in parts of north and south-eastern Bihar on April 24 and 25, which may bring temporary relief from the prevailing heat.
Heatwave impacts daily life
The intense heat has begun to disrupt normal life in several districts. In Rohtas, people were seen covering their heads to shield themselves from the scorching sun, while in Madhubani, roads remained largely deserted during peak afternoon hours.
Students affected by extreme heat
In Buxar, 10 students reportedly fainted due to the extreme heat and were admitted to hospital. The district recorded temperatures close to 42°C. Heatwave-like conditions prevailed in at least five districts, raising concerns over public health.
Officials have advised vulnerable groups, including children, the elderly, and those with pre-existing illnesses, to take adequate precautions.
Over the past 24 hours, 11 districts in Bihar recorded maximum temperatures of 40°C or above. Apart from Rohtas, Kaimur also reported a high of 43°C.
In separate incidents, several schoolgirls fainted in Buxar, while a student in Jamui fell ill and had to be made to lie down in class.
Patna braces for hotter day
Patna is expected to witness another day of intense heat, with the maximum temperature likely to reach 41°C. Strong sunlight and hot winds are forecast during the afternoon hours.
Authorities have urged residents to avoid unnecessary outdoor activity during peak heat hours.
Nalanda likely to touch 41°C
In neighbouring Nalanda district, clear skies are expected to prevail, with temperatures likely to range between 26°C and 41°C. Low wind speeds of around 5 kmph are offering little respite from the heat.
The continuing rise in temperatures across Bihar has raised concerns about prolonged heatwave conditions in the coming days.
